text.title: Test Pyramid vs Testing Trophy: Per-Module Decision Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Test Pyramid vs Testing Trophy: Per-Module Decision methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Decision MUST answer all 5 questions: stack-shape, change-frequency, integration-risk, runtime-budget, ac-grain. Skipping any question = invalid record.
rule.rationale: Decision MUST answer all 5 questions: stack-shape, change-frequency, integration-risk, runtime-budget, ac-grain. Skipping any question = invalid record.

rule.statement: Decision is recorded PER MODULE. Repos with mixed workloads MUST NOT pick a single global shape.
rule.rationale: Decision is recorded PER MODULE. Repos with mixed workloads MUST NOT pick a single global shape.

rule.statement: Each module record names current unit + integration + e2e runtimes in seconds. Without baseline numbers, rebalance cannot be measured.
rule.rationale: Each module record names current unit + integration + e2e runtimes in seconds. Without baseline numbers, rebalance cannot be measured.

rule.statement: Each record names a single owner accountable for the module's test shape. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Each record names a single owner accountable for the module's test shape. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.

rule.statement: Each record carries a next_review_date ≤90d from last_reviewed. Stale decisions auto-flag for review.
rule.rationale: Each record carries a next_review_date ≤90d from last_reviewed. Stale decisions auto-flag for review.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-qa-test-pyramid-vs-trophy-decision.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
